name: cerebrium

metadata.name: cerebrium
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Affero General Public License v3

documentation:
# Cerebrium CLI

Cerebrium is the Python package built for use with the [Cerebrium](https://www.cerebrium.ai/) platform. 
It allows you to deploy machine learning apps as a REST API with a single line of code.

For usage consult the [documentation](https://docs.cerebrium.ai/). The repo for the documentation can be found [here](https://github.com/CerebriumAI/docs).

## Releases

The CLI follows semver. Commits should be tagged accordingly:

allowed_types = [
    "build", "chore", "ci", "docs", "feat", "fix",
    "perf", "style", "refactor", "test"
]
minor_types = ["feat"]
patch_types = ["fix", "perf"]

# Testing:

Testing in this repository is performed using pytest.

All tests for the cli-package are located in tests/ in the root directory of this repository.

To run tests, run the following command in the root directory:

1. export PYTHONPATH="${PYTHONPATH}:${pwd}"
2. poetry run pytest tests/unit

This will run all tests in the tests/ directory.

## Notes:

- If you want to run a specific test, you can do so by providing the path to the test file as an argument to pytest. For
  example, to run the test in tests/unit/test_cli.py, you can run the following command:

```bash
poetry run pytest tests/unit/test_cli.py
```

- If you would like to see all printed output from the tests, you can add the -s flag to the pytest command. For
  example:

```bash
poetry run pytest -s tests/unit/test_cli.py
```

- When writing tests:
  - Ensure there are at least one positive and one negative test.
  - Please ensure that the test is isolated from the rest of the system. This means that the test should not rely on any external resources or the state of the system.
  - All CLI tests should be run-able in a temporary directory and there should be no side effects.

## How to run locally

```bash
poetry install
poetry shell
export CEREBRIUM_ENV=dev
which cerebrium
```

this should point to cerebrium-cli/.venv/bin/cerebrium


## How to run PyRight Linter

```bash
poetry run pyright
```

functions.name: cerebrium.main.version
documentation: Print the version of the Cerebrium CLI
cli.command("run")(run_user_app)

variables.name: cerebrium.defaults.RESPONSE_GRACE_PERIOD
documentation: 15 minutes

functions.name: cerebrium.api.is_logged_in
documentation:
Check if a user's JWT token has expired. If it has, make a request to Cognito with the refresh token to generate a new one.

Returns:
    str: The new JWT token if the old one has expired, otherwise the current JWT token.

functions.name: cerebrium.api.cerebrium_request
documentation:
Make a request to the Cerebrium API and check the response for errors.

Args:
    http_method ('GET', 'POST', 'DELETE'): The HTTP method to use (GET, POST or DELETE).
    url (str): The url after the base url to use.
    payload (dict, optional): The payload to send with the request.
    requires_auth (bool): If the api call requires the user to be authenticated
    headers (dict, optional): By default, content-type is application/json so this is used to override
    v1 (bool, optional): If the request is to the v1 API. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    dict: The response from the request.

functions.name: cerebrium.api.fetch_build_logs
documentation:
Fetches the build logs from the API.

Args:
    app_name (str): The app name.
    build_id (str): The build ID.

Returns:
    dict: The response JSON containing the logs and build status.
Function to get the logs from the API

functions.name: cerebrium.commands.logs.display_logs
documentation:
Display logs in a table format.

Args:
    logs_data (List[Dict]): A list of log entries.

functions.name: cerebrium.commands.logs.fetch_logs
documentation:
Fetch logs for the specified app or run.

Args:
    project_id (str): The project ID.
    app_id (str): The application ID.
    run_id (Optional[str]): The run ID for fetching specific logs (if applicable).
    last_log_timestamp (Optional[str]): The timestamp after which to fetch logs.

Returns:
    Tuple[List[Dict], Optional[str]]: A tuple containing the list of log entries and the latest timestamp.

functions.name: cerebrium.commands.files.ls_files
documentation:
List contents of persistent storage. Run `cerebrium ls --help` for more information.



Usage: cerebrium ls [OPTIONS] [REMOTE_PATH]



  List contents of persistent storage.



Options:

  -h, --help          Show this message and exit.



Examples:

  # List all files in the root directory

  cerebrium ls



  # List all files in a specific directory

  cerebrium ls sub_folder/

functions.name: cerebrium.commands.files.cp_file
documentation:
Copy contents to persistent storage. Run `cerebrium cp --help` for more information.



Usage: cerebrium cp [OPTIONS] LOCAL_PATH REMOTE_PATH (Optional)



  Copy contents to persistent storage.



Options:

  -h, --help          Show this message and exit.



Examples:

  # Copy a single file

  cerebrium cp src_file_name.txt # copies to /src_file_name.txt



  cerebrium cp src_file_name.txt dest_file_name.txt # copies to /dest_file_name.txt



  # Copy a directory

  cerebrium cp dir_name # copies to the root directory

  cerebrium cp dir_name sub_folder/ # copies to sub_folder/

functions.name: cerebrium.commands.files.rm_file
documentation:
Remove a file or directory from persistent storage. Run `cerebrium rm --help` for more information.



Usage: cerebrium rm [OPTIONS] REMOTE_PATH



  Remove a file or directory from persistent storage.



Options:

  -h, --help          Show this message and exit.



Examples:

  # Remove a specific file

  cerebrium rm /file_name.txt

  cerebrium rm /sub_folder/file_name.txt



  # Remove a directory and all its contents

  cerebrium rm /sub_folder/ # Note that it must end with a forward slash /

  cerebrium rm / # Removes all files in the root directory

functions.name: cerebrium.utils.project.is_valid_project_id
documentation: Validate that the project ID starts with 'p-' or 'dev-p-'

functions.name: cerebrium.utils.project.get_current_project_context

functions.name: cerebrium.utils.deploy.format_created_at
documentation:
Formats the created_at timestamp to a nice local time format.

Args:
    created_at (str): The original timestamp.

Returns:
    str: The formatted local time string.

functions.name: cerebrium.utils.deploy.poll_build_logs
documentation:
Polls logs at specified intervals and prints only new log lines. Handles the polling of logs with optional spinner and status handling.

Args:
    build_id (str): The unique identifier of the build.
    cerebrium_config (CerebriumConfig): The Cerebrium configuration object.
    build_status (str): The current build status.
    disable_animation (bool): Flag to disable the spinner animation.
    interval (int): The interval in seconds between polls. Defaults to 2 seconds.

Returns:
    str: The final build status.

functions.name: cerebrium.utils.deploy.get_function_names
documentation:
Extracts function names and methods from a Python file.
- If `asgi` is True, looks for FastAPI-style decorators and extracts the method and the function name.
- If `asgi` is False, extracts function names and assigns a default method ('POST').

Returns:
    List of tuples (method, function_name).

functions.name: cerebrium.utils.logging.cerebrium_log
documentation:
User-friendly colored logging

Args:
    message (str): Error message to be displayed
    prefix (str): Prefix to be displayed. Defaults to empty.
    level (LogLevel): Log level. Defaults to "INFO".
    attrs (list, optional): Attributes for colored printing. Defaults to None.
    color (Color, optional): Color to print in. Defaults depending on log level.
    prefix_separator (str, optional): Separator between prefix and message. Defaults to "   ".
    spinner (Yaspin, optional): Spinner object to write to. Defaults to None.

variables.name: cerebrium.utils.logging.error_messages
documentation: Error messages to check for

functions.name: cerebrium.utils.termination._graceful_shutdown
documentation:
This function is called when the user presses Ctrl+C while streaming logs.
- stops the spinner
- sends a kill signal to the backend to stop the build job
- prints a message
- exits the program
